title: Push pin with antirotational stopper and method of use thereof
abstract: A push pin with rotational stopper and method of use thereof includes a push pin configured with a plurality of retention barbs extending outward from a surface of the insertion member, the insertion member configured with an anti-rotation section to friction fit therewith a sidewall of an aperture in the panel, an disc spring positioned proximate said anti-rotation section and configured to provide an opposing force to said retention barbs to limit insertion and removal therein the aperture in the panel.
claims: Claims CLAIMS Therefore, at least the following is claimed: 1. A push pin for insertion into an aperture in a panel, said push pin comprising: an elongated member, said elongated member having a first member end and a second member end configured with one or more retention barbs extending outward from said elongated member and positioned therebetween said first member end and said second member end; a three or more sided anti-rotation section positioned proximate and integral thereto said first member end; and a disc spring positioned proximate and integral thereto said three or more sided anti- rotation section opposite said elongated member. 2. The push pm of claim 1, wherein said one or more retention barbs configured on an acute angle relative to said elongated member from said second member end to said first member end. 3. The push pin of claim 2, wherein said disc spring configured on an obtuse angle relative to said elongated member from said second member end to said first member end. 4. The push pin of claim 3, wherein said one or more retention barbs provides a counter removal force and said disc spring provides a counter insertion force to hold the push pin therein the aperture in the panel. 5. The push pin of claim 4, wherein said counter removal force opposes said counter insertion force. 6. The push pin of claim 1, wherein said three or more sided anti-rotation section configured as a polygon. 7. The push pin of claim 1, wherein said three or more sided anti-rotation section configured as a star. 8. The push pin of claim 1 , wherein said three or more sided anti-rotation section configured as an "H". 9. The push pin of claim 1 , wherein said three or more sided anti-rotation section configuration is selected from the group consisting of a triangle, a pentagon, a hexagon, a heptagon, an octagon, a nonagon, a decagon, a cross, a square, a rectangle, a rhombus, a trapezoid, a parallelogram, and a plus. 10. The push pin of claim 1 , further comprises a head positioned proximate and integral thereto said disc spring. 11. The push pin of claim 10, wherein said head further comprises a USCAR slot connector. 12. The push pin of claim 11, wherein said USCAR slot connector further comprises a plurality of sidewalls with a latch positioned therein. 13. The push pin of claim 10, wherein said head further comprises a USCAR clip connector. 14. The push pin of claim 13, wherein said USCAR clip connector further comprises a mounting base having a retention arm with first retention arm end affixed thereto said base and a second retention arm end extending therefrom and having a retention barb. 15. The push pin of claim 10, wherein said head further comprises a cable tie. 16. The push pin of claim 15, wherein said cable tie further comprises an elongated strap and a locking buckle. 17. The push pin of claim 16, wherein said elongated strap further comprises a plurality of cross-bars and said locking buckle further comprises a channel having a pawl positioned therein, said pawl configured to engage said plurality of cross-bars. 18. The push pin of claim 10, wherein said head further comprises an alternate cable tie having a head with a channel. 19. The push pin of claim 18, wherein said alternate cable tie further comprises an elongated strap having one or more rails configured to run from approximately a first strap end to a second strap end. 20. The push pin of claim 19, wherein said one or more rails and said channel is configured to accommodate passage therethrough of said strap. 21. The cable tie of claim 20, wherein said strap further comprises one or more longitudinally spaced aperture positioned between said one or more rails. 22. The cable tie of claim 21 , wherein said one or more rails further comprises one or more cross-bars and said channel further comprises one or more pawls positioned therein to confront individually associated ones of said one or more cross-bars. 23. A method of fastening an electrical component to an aperture in a panel, said method comprising the steps of: providing a push pin having an elongated member, said elongated member having a first member end and a second member end configured with one or more retention barbs extending outward from said elongated member and positioned therebetween said first member end and said second member end, a three or more sided anti-rotation section positioned proximate and integral thereto said first member end, a disc spring positioned proximate and integral thereto said three or more sided anti-rotation section opposite said elongated member; inserting said elongated member therein the aperture of the panel; twisting said push pin to align said three or more sided anti-rotation section with the aperture of the panel; and inserting said three or more sided anti-rotation section therein the aperture of the panel until said disc spring contacts the panel. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising the step of providing a head positioned proximate and integral thereto said disc spring. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein said head further comprises a USCA slot connector. 26. The method of claim 25, further comprising the step of connecting the electrical component to said USCAR slot connector. 27. The method of claim 24, wherein said head further comprises a USCAR clip connector. 28. The method of claim 27, further comprising the step of connecting the electrical component to said USCAR clip connector.
